tactical_shape: Used a transformative triple substitution to change the game
Played with width and pressure, with Dan Ndoye repeatedly running Bosnia ragged on the left
Relied on substitutes to inject pace and quality in the final third
standout_players: Johan Manzambi: player of the match; scored twice from the bench, brought poise and changed the match
Dan Ndoye: gave Bosnia the runaround and was Switzerland's most likely outlet before the breakthrough
Granit Xhaka: scored the final goal from the penalty spot
Rubén Vargas: came off the bench and scored
Breel Embolo: won and converted a penalty in the earlier match context mentioned; here was released on goal by Manzambi
home_read.momentum: World Cup now truly up and running; in a position of power going into the Canada game.

tactical_shape: Stubborn, compact opponents who relied on set pieces as their main route to goal
Spent much of the match under pressure, with attacks breaking down in the final action
Went down to 10 men after Tarik Muharemovic's red card
standout_players: Edin Dzeko: the focal point up front; had a first-half chance and was later withdrawn after a booking
Kerim Alajbegovic: created Bosnia's first real sight of goal with a pass to Dzeko
Nikola Katic: notable for his battling defending and winning a goal-kick after resisting a Swiss attack
Ermin Mahmic: scored a stoppage-time consolation with an unstoppable volley
fatigue_injuries: Tarik Muharemovic sent off after lunging in on Embolo
Amar Dedic booked for tugging at Ndoye
Edin Dzeko booked and then withdrawn
away_read.momentum: First defeat since last September; knockout hopes now hinge on beating Qatar in the final group game.
